questions.question: Explain the difference between pointers and references in C++ with examples.
expectedPoints: Pointers are variables that store memory addresses, references are aliases to existing variables
Pointers can be reassigned, references cannot
Pointers can be null, references cannot
Pointers require dereferencing with *, references behave like normal variables
Pointers support arithmetic, references do not

questions.question: Compare and contrast pass-by-pointer and pass-by-reference for function parameters. Include examples and discuss when each approach is appropriate.
expectedPoints: Syntax differences: pointers use *, references use &
Pointer parameters can be null, reference parameters cannot
References provide cleaner syntax without dereferencing
Pointers allow optional parameters through null values
References are generally preferred for mandatory parameters
Pointers are needed when reassigning the pointer itself or when null is a valid state
Performance is similar as both pass addresses
Examples of swap function using both approaches
